raw_consumer_statement: I'm still in a bad situation with citibank. I have a line of credit/credit card with Citibank. I lost my credit card on XX/XX/year>. The last transaction was made at a XXXX XXXX  station on XX/XX/year> in XXXX, NY. Several days later a fraudulent transaction was made for {$6200.00} from XXXX in XXXX, NY. On the day the fraudulent transaction was made, I received a call from Citibank and immediately spoke to a representative from Citibank to verify the purchase. When I reviewed the summary of charges with the representative, she found a transaction for {$6200.00} from XXXX in XXXX, NY that was charged to my card which I did not authorize on XX/XX/year>. I told the representative that the card was lost several days before the day the fraud transaction was made and that the purchase was fraudulent. My last transaction was made on XX/XX/XXXX at a gas station in XXXX, NY. The representative issued me a new card and then told me that the fraud transaction would later be removed. The charge was still posted to my account. I spoke to a representative from Citibank for a second time on XX/XX/year>. I explained my situation about the fraudulent transaction again. I cooperated with the investigation and provided information to the Citibank representative. I was told again not to worry and that the fraud charge of {$6200.00} would immediately be removed. As of now, the charge is still on my account. This was different and opposed to what was originally assured to me. The charge is unusual and uncharacteristic with the history of charges made on my Citibank credit card. I have not ever made CC charges with the XXXX merchant in the past. The unauthorized fraud charge of {$6200.00} from XXXX is still on my Citibank account. This unfortunate circumstance is a huge inconvenience to me. If this situation isnt handled properly I will never bank or do business with Citibank again.
